_meta.purpose: Canonical AWS-service -> GCP-service classification (the 'logical construct'). Single source of truth used by service_classifier.py to force the correct GCP target and eliminate LLM label variance. Grounded in Google's official 'Google Cloud for AWS professionals' service comparison. Curate this file to add/adjust services.

mode_semantics.keep: Pipeline already prices this well deterministically — classifier only STAMPS metadata (category/reason), never changes pricing or the mapped SKU.
mode_semantics.review: Correct GCP target is known but precise GCP pricing needs per-service modelling. Classifier FORCES gcp_service=target + strategy=passthrough (carry AWS cost, no invented figure) + manual-review flag. Honest baseline until a deterministic pricer is built.
mode_semantics.ignore: Not a real workload cost on GCP (burstable CPU credits, billing fees, taxes, support, refunds). Classifier FORCES strategy='ignore' -> $0. GCP has no equivalent charge.

_meta.match: Case-insensitive substring against the AWS product name with the leading 'Amazon '/'AWS ' stripped. First match wins, so order specific -> general.
_meta.field_order_note: Downstream row metadata stored as: category, gcp_service, confidence, rule='service_map_v1', reason (in projection_note).

rules.reason: Audit/API logging; priced per log volume ingested — needs sizing.
Multi-component (metrics + logs + alarms + dashboards). Single-SKU mapping under-projected badly (~0.07x); passthrough baseline until component pricer built.
Multi-component (gateway-hours + data-processed $/GB). Single-component mapping under-projected (~0.5x); passthrough baseline until component pricer built.
Cloud NAT: gateway-hours + data processed.
AWS Config has no 1:1 GCP equivalent (partial: Asset Inventory).
Managed Hadoop/Spark -> Dataproc; size by cluster node hours.
Serverless ETL -> Dataflow (or Cloud Data Fusion).
Serverless query -> BigQuery on-demand (per TB scanned).
Data warehouse -> BigQuery (or BigQuery editions/Dataproc).
Streaming -> Pub/Sub (or Managed Kafka for KDS-heavy).
MSK -> Managed Kafka; size by broker vCPU/RAM/storage.
MSK -> Managed Kafka.
No managed GCP equivalent; self-host on GCE/GKE — needs sizing.
No managed GCP equivalent.
BI -> Looker / Looker Studio Pro (per-user); needs seat sizing.
EKS cluster management fee ($0.10/hr/cluster) -> GKE Standard cluster fee ($0.10/hr). Underlying EC2 node costs appear as separate EC2 line items.
ECS Fargate tasks -> GKE Autopilot pod capacity. ECS on EC2 nodes are billed as EC2 — those appear as separate EC2 line items.
Fargate vCPU/memory hours -> GKE Autopilot pod CPU/memory resources (directional estimate).
FaaS -> Cloud Run / Cloud Functions (per request + vCPU-sec).

Redis/Memcached -> Memorystore; size by node GB + tier. Verify cluster mode: ElastiCache Redis Cluster mode maps to Memorystore Redis Cluster (different SKU, 30-40% higher). Confidence reduced from 88 to 78 until cluster topology is confirmed.
DAX -> Memorystore for Redis.
DAX -> Memorystore for Redis.
Key-value/doc -> Firestore (simple access patterns) or Bigtable (GSI-heavy / analytical). Target is workload-dependent; GSI-heavy DynamoDB requires re-architecture on GCP. Confidence capped at 50%.
Graph DB; no managed GCP equivalent — self-host or Spanner Graph.
Mongo-compatible; no managed GCP equivalent (MongoDB Atlas / Firestore).
DMS -> Database Migration Service.
SNS -> Pub/Sub.
SQS -> Pub/Sub.
SES has no native GCP equivalent (3rd-party/Workspace).
NFS file storage -> Filestore; price per provisioned GB + tier.
AWS Managed AD -> Managed Service for Microsoft AD.
Native billing/reporting is free in GCP — likely $0.
KMS -> Cloud KMS (per key-version + operations).
-> Secret Manager (per secret-version + access).
Threat detection -> SCC Premium (different pricing model).
VPC Endpoint -> Private Service Connect (per endpoint + data).
Transit Gateway -> NCC hub (per attachment + data).
VPN -> Cloud VPN (per tunnel-hour + egress).
VPN -> Cloud VPN.
Resolver endpoints (DNS resolution/forwarding) != authoritative managed zone; needs Cloud DNS policies / hybrid DNS — architecture review.
Route 53 Resolver endpoint -> Cloud DNS forwarding, not a managed zone.
Public IPv4 -> External IP charge (per IP-hour).
Generic VPC networking -> VPC (most sub-features free; verify component).
Elemental MediaConvert -> Transcoder API (per output minute).
Live video; no clean GCP equivalent.
SMS/Pinpoint; no native GCP equivalent (3rd-party).
Pinpoint; no native GCP equivalent.

rules.comment: --- ignore ($0): not a workload cost on GCP ---

rules.reason: Burstable CPU credits; GCP E2/shared-core has no credit charge.
Late-payment fee; not a cloud resource cost.
Fee; not a resource cost.
Support plan; priced separately on GCP, not a per-SKU cost.
Tax line; not a resource cost.
Refund/credit adjustment; not a resource cost.
AWS negotiated discount; nets AWS total down. GCP has no equivalent — GCP side stays at list ($0 offset).
AWS EDP discount; AWS-only, not a GCP cost.
